Package org.jlab.epsci.ersap.std.cli

Class Config

java.lang.Object
org.jlab.epsci.ersap.std.cli.Config
public class Config extends Object
Environment configuration for a ERSAP shell session.

  • Nested Class Summary

    Nested Classes
    Modifier and Type
    Class
    Description
    static class 
    Config.Builder
    Helps creating the configuration for a shell session.

    Fields
    Modifier and Type
    Field
    Description
    static final String
    DESCRIPTION
    The variable for the data processing description keyword.
    static final String
    FILES_LIST
    The variable for the list of input files.
    static final String
    FRONTEND_HOST
    The variable for the address of the front-end DPE.

    FRONTEND_PORT
    The variable for the port of the front-end DPE.
    static final String
    INPUT_DIR
    The variable for the input directory.
    static final String
    JAVA_MEMORY
    The variable for the the JVM heap size used by the DPE.
    static final String
    JAVA_OPTIONS
    The variable for the JVM options of the Java DPE (overrides JAVA_MEMORY).
    static final String
    LOG_DIR
    The variable for the log directory.
    static final String
    MAX_EVENTS
    The variable for the number events to be processed from the input file.
    static final String
    MAX_THREADS
    The variable for the number of data processing threads.
    static final String
    MONITOR_HOST
    The variable for the address of the monitoring front-end.
    static final String
    OUT_FILE_PREFIX
    The variable for the output file prefix.

    OUTPUT_DIR
    The variable for the output directory.
    static final String
    REPORT_EVENTS
    The variable for the report frequency of completed events.
    static final String
    SERVICES_FILE
    The variable for the orchestrator configuration file.
    static final String
    SESSION
    The variable for the session of the ERSAP DPE.
    static final String
    SKIP_EVENTS
    The variable for the number events to skip from the input file.

  • Method Details

    • hasVariable

      public boolean hasVariable(String name)
      Checks if a variable of the given name exists.
      Parameters:
      name - the variable to check
      Returns:
      true if there is a variable with the given name, false otherwise

    • hasValue

      public boolean hasValue(String variable)
      Checks if the given variable has a value.
      Parameters:
      variable - the variable to check
      Returns:
      true if there is a variable has a value, false otherwise
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the variable does not exist

    • getValue

      public Object getValue(String variable)
      Gets the value of the specified variable.
      Parameters:
      variable - the name of the variable
      Returns:
      the current value of the variable, if set
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the variable does not exist
      IllegalStateException - if the variable has no value

    • getString

      public String getString(String variable)
      Get the string value of a variable.
      Parameters:
      variable - the name of the variable
      Returns:
      the current string value of the variable, if set

      Get the integer value of a variable.

      the current integer value of the variable, if set

      Get the long value of a variable.

      Returns:
      the current long value of the variable, if set

      public double getDouble(String variable)
      Get the double value of a variable.
      Parameters:
      variable - the name of the variable
      Returns:
      the current long value of the variable, if set

    • getBoolean

      public boolean getBoolean(String variable)
      Get the boolean value of a variable.
      Parameters:
      variable - the name of the variable
      Returns:
      the current boolean value of the variable, if set

    • getenv

      public Map<String,String> getenv()
      Returns an unmodifiable string map view of the custom environment variables set for the shell. These are extra variables to the system environment, defined when the shell was created.

      The variables should be added to the inherited environment when starting a subprocess: 

       ProcessBuilder b = new ProcessBuilder(command);
 b.environment().putAll(config.getenv());
 b.start()
      Returns:
      the custom extra environment as a map of variable names to values
